stimulus: Anthropologist Joaquín Herrera explains why the seventh-century stepped platform at El Pinar in northern Honduras was likely the funerary monument of a regional ruler. First, the imported jadeite ornaments arranged on the platform suggest that the person interred there was a wealthy and influential leader. ______blank the enormous labor required to quarry and stack the platform's limestone blocks would almost certainly have been mustered only for a ruler.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Second" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that the labor required to build the platform would only have been mustered for a ruler—joins the previous sentence (introduced by "First") in supporting Herrera's claim that the monument was likely the funerary site of a regional ruler.

Choice A is incorrect because "instead" illogically signals that this sentence presents an alternative to the previous information about the jadeite ornaments, when it actually adds a second piece of supporting evidence. Choice B is incorrect because "still" illogically signals a contrast with the previous information, when this sentence in fact reinforces Herrera's claim. Choice D is incorrect because "specifically" illogically signals that this sentence elaborates on the previous one about the ornaments, when it instead introduces a separate, additional piece of evidence.
